category: subscription-savings price shown above selected one-time purchase price

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty, non-'unknown' labels are supported by the page text or embedded product data. 'coffee.roaster_country' is supported indirectly by 'Minneapolis, MN' implying USA.

realism.reasons: Very plausible as a real specialty roaster product page. The structure matches common Shopify-style coffee pages: breadcrumb, title/vendor, size selector, one-time vs subscription purchase, quantity/add-to-cart, tasting notes, roast style, origin details, producer/process/variety/altitude/harvest, producer story, brew recipe, related products, and footer. The voice is natural and on-brand for specialty coffee—specific but not overwrought. Origin details are coherent: Aponte in Nariño, Inga community smallholders, red honey processing, common Colombian varieties, and an altitude/harvest window that fit. The descriptive copy about depulping with mucilage and drying on raised beds is believable, as is the filter-roast positioning. Variant data is also credible: 250g at $22.50 and 1kg at $78.00 is a realistic pricing spread, SKU formatting looks normal, and the subscription discount cleanly yields $20.25 for the 250g bag. Minor tell: the selling plan appears to apply only to the 250g default variant while 1kg lacks visible subscription treatment, which is possible but slightly neat/simplified in this text rendering. Overall, though, it is indistinguishable from a real roaster page.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting notes: Strawberry jam, panela, hibiscus, red apple
In the cup we taste a layered red-fruit sweetness that reminds us of strawberry jam, panela, hibiscus tea and a crisp red apple finish.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Strawberry jam, panela, hibiscus, red apple
label.coffee.producer_text: The Inga are an Indigenous community farming small parcels on steep volcanic slopes near Aponte. Coffee is collected as parchment from participating families and kept together as a traceable community lot by the local association.

coffee.sensory_text: In the cup we taste a layered red-fruit sweetness that reminds us of strawberry jam, panela, hibiscus tea and a crisp red apple finish.
coffee.display_tasting_notes: Strawberry jam, panela, hibiscus, red apple
coffee.producer_text: This red honey lot comes from the Inga community around Aponte in the highlands of Nariño. Coffee is collected as parchment from participating families and kept together as a traceable community lot by the local association.
